title: 'Not acceptable to us': Pakistan shoots down Donald Trump’s Abraham Accords call
content: Pakistan has firmly rejected joining the Abraham Accords, with Defence Minister Khawaja Asif stating it contradicts fundamental ideologies. Islamabad's long-standing stance is clear: no recognition of Israel without a Palestinian state. This comes as former US President Donald Trump urged several Muslim-majority nations to normalize ties with Israel, linking it to Iran negotiations.

title: 'Distress' over abortion, Rs 7 lakh claim, going off radar: What Twisha Sharma’s husband told cops
content: Prime accused Samarth Singh told investigators his wife, model-turned-actor Twisha Sharma, was distressed after an abortion and claimed he gave her Rs 7 lakh. He attempted to mislead police about his whereabouts post-death. His mother, a former judge, is also being questioned. The Supreme Court has directed the CBI to take over the probe.

title: ‘Give me a break, you muppet’: Gillespie tears into Pietersen over IPL vs county row
content: A heated debate over the IPL versus county cricket has erupted, with Jason Gillespie launching a scathing attack on Kevin Pietersen. Pietersen criticised Alastair Cook's suggestion that young talent Jacob Bethell should leave the IPL 2026 for county cricket, but Gillespie defended Cook's point about the importance of game time.

title: Karnataka power tussle reaches Delhi: Siddaramaiah, DKS hold key meet with Congress top brass
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has been summoned to Delhi for a crucial meeting with the party high command amid ongoing speculation about a leadership transition. While party leaders attribute the visit to routine consultations for upcoming Rajya Sabha and MLC elections, the internal friction between Siddaramaiah and Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar is expected to be a key discussion point.

title: Akshay, Amitabh rode in his auto: Meet auto driver who offers free jaggery & peanuts to passengers
content: Satyawan Gite's auto-rickshaw in Mumbai transcends a mere ride, transforming into a mobile haven equipped with amenities like a wash basin, phone charging, and even healthy snacks.  His dedication to passenger comfort and safety, including special care for senior citizens and women, has earned him widespread appreciation and memorable encounters with celebrities and tourists alike.

title: Watch: Sooryavanshi gives Parag an 'awkward' stare after 'hum nahi hai film star' remark
content: A viral airport video of Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag and teenage star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi amused fans after Parag joked, “we're not flim stars!” and Vaibhav reacted with an awkward stare. The clip surfaced after RR reached the IPL 2026 playoffs. Coach Kumar Sangakkara backed Parag’s leadership despite criticism, while Vaibhav said he aims to break Chris Gayle’s T20 record and score 200.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Cops in civilian clothes seized hard disks: How drama unfolded at Abhishek Banerjee’s home
content: Kolkata police visited Abhishek Banerjee's Shantiniketan residence. Plainclothes officers removed a monitor and hard disks. This follows a reduction in Banerjee's security cover. Trinamool claims it was a routine removal of security equipment. Police stated it was a standard official exercise. The incident has sparked speculation.

title: Arshdeep Singh deletes more than 200 posts, including viral Virat Kohli video
content: Arshdeep Singh sparked online speculation after deleting over 200 Instagram posts, including his viral Champions Trophy reel with Virat Kohli, and changing his profile picture without explanation. The move came after several IPL 2026 controversies, including backlash over an alleged racial remark and social media spats. Arshdeep also endured a disappointing season, taking 14 wickets with an economy rate above 10.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Chunky defends Ananya for brutal trolling on her ‘Bharatanatyam’ moves
content: Ananya Panday faced online backlash for her Bharatanatyam-inspired dance in 'Chand Mera Dil'. Her father, Chunky Panday, defended her, clarifying it was a fusion performance, not a classical recital. He emphasized that pure Bharatanatyam demands extensive training and urged viewers to understand the film's context before judging the creative blend.

title: HC refuses interim order on Delhi Gymkhana Club takeover notice; Centre says no forcible possession
content: The Delhi High Court has been assured by the Centre that there will be no forcible eviction from the Delhi Gymkhana Club premises on June 5. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta stated that any action will be taken strictly as per law and with prior notice.

title: Eid Al-Adha 2026 date: Is Bakrid on May 27 or 28? Here’s what changed after moon wasn’t sighted
content: Confusion over Bakrid 2026 dates is resolved: most of India will celebrate Eid Al-Adha on Thursday, May 28, following a delayed moon sighting. Some regions, like Jammu and Kashmir, will observe it on May 27 due to local traditions. This shift has led to revised government holidays and institutional schedules nationwide.

title: Minnesota Vikings, Dallas Cowboys linked in surprise Linebacker trade rumors
content: Blake Cashman has emerged as a potential trade candidate as the Minnesota Vikings enter the final year of his contract. The Dallas Cowboys have been suggested as a possible landing spot, with analysts believing a Day 3 draft pick could get a deal done. Despite the speculation, Cashman remains one of Minnesota's most productive defenders after recording 144 tackles in 13 games last season, making a trade far from certain.

title: Blake Lively ‘convinced’ Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce won’t snub her wedding invite
content: Blake Lively is reportedly optimistic that her friendship with Taylor Swift can recover after the Justin Baldoni controversy and settlement. Insiders claim Lively already has a dress prepared for Swift’s future wedding because she believes she will still be invited. The rumors reignited debate online about tension inside Swift’s celebrity circle. While some sources say Taylor remains cautious about her guest list, others believe removing the legal drama could create space for reconciliation between the longtime friends moving forward.

title: Tyreek Hill shocks fans with new career move amid NFL free agency silence
content: Free agent wide receiver Tyreek Hill has embraced DJing in Miami while recovering from knee surgery. He finds enjoyment in EDM music. Despite being linked to several teams for several months since his release from the Miami Dolphins, no NFL franchise has signed him as of yet. Hill’s new hobby could potentially enhance his football skills. The incident mirrors the positive impact seen with Bijan Robinson’s DJing experience in 2024.

title: Can a husband divorce his wife for not cooking? Bombay high court answers
content: The Bombay High Court declared that wives are not "deemed maids" and cannot be accused of cruelty for imperfect household chores. Overturning a divorce order, the court mandated a husband to pay Rs 20,000 monthly maintenance, emphasizing marriage as an equal partnership, not a service contract.  Cruelty requires severe conduct, not minor marital disagreements.

title: India-US ink rare earth pact as China concerns grow: How New Delhi could benefit
content: India and the US have signed a bilateral framework to secure supplies, mining, and processing of critical minerals and rare earth elements. This agreement aims to strengthen resilient and diversified supply chains, fostering collaboration on financing and management of these vital resources. The pact underscores the strategic importance of the growing partnership between the two nations.

title: How Russia’s Kh-101 missile uses decoy flares to slip past air defence shields
content: Russia is upgrading its Kh-101 cruise missiles with advanced countermeasures to counter Ukraine's air defences. Recent footage shows these missiles deploying flares and potentially radar-confusing chaff. Despite these efforts, Ukraine claims high interception rates, though Russia's hypersonic missiles like Oreshnik pose a greater future threat.

title: KKR IPL 2026 review: Why Rahane’s side never recovered from their horror start
content: Kolkata Knight Riders faced a disappointing IPL 2026 season, missing the playoffs for the second consecutive year. A winless start and injuries to key players like Matheesha Pathirana and Mustafizur Rahman significantly impacted the team. Despite a strong fightback, KKR could not overcome the early damage, finishing seventh. Youngsters Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Kartik Tyagi emerged as positives.

title: Explained: Why Taiwan has overtaken India to become world’s fifth largest stock market
content: According to data compiled by Bloomberg, Taiwan’s overall market capitalisation rose to $4.95 trillion as of Monday, while India’s market value declined to $4.92 trillion.

title: Why US struck Iran amid shaky ceasefire: 10 things to know about the 'self-defence' strikes
content: US forces struck Iranian missile sites and boats near the Strait of Hormuz. This happened during peace talks aimed at ending a three-month conflict. Iran accused the US of a war crime. Reports mentioned explosions and fatalities on Larak Island. Discussions continue on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's uranium stockpile.

title: TOI Poll: Sooryavanshi voted breakout star; 86% say IPL is too batting-friendly
content: A TOI Online poll reveals Royal Challengers Bengaluru as IPL 2026 playoff favourites, backed by 39% of fans. Young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi emerged as the standout talent, securing 89% of votes for most promising player. Concerns about batting dominance and technology's impact on game flow were also highlighted by a significant majority of respondents.

title: Rupee slips to 95.43 against US dollar as hope for Middle East peace dims
content: The Indian Rupee experienced a dip early Tuesday, influenced by strong US dollar demand and rising crude oil prices. Geopolitical tensions and fresh attacks against Iran are impacting market sentiment. While the Reserve Bank of India's interventions are expected to stabilize the currency, ongoing peace talks and oil price fluctuations remain key factors to watch.

title: 'Is this Tamil Nadu or UP?': Udhayanidhi Stalin, EPS slam TVK govt over rising crime
content: Opposition leaders Udhayanidhi Stalin and Edappadi K Palaniswami have strongly criticized the current government over a surge in crime across Tamil Nadu. They highlighted recent murders and sexual offenses, questioning the administration's handling of law and order and accusing the ruling party of creating a false sense of change amidst escalating violence.

title: Farhan Akhtar in talks to play Pakistani cricketer in Aamir Khan’s next film
content: Filmmaker Farhan Akhtar is reportedly considering an acting role in Ashutosh Gowariker's upcoming period drama about cricket icon Lala Amarnath, set during India's Partition.  Akhtar, also producing, might play a pivotal cameo as Amarnath's close friend. This project reunites Aamir Khan and Gowariker after 'Lagaan'.

title: IPL Qualifier 1: Away from Kohli vs Gill spotlight, Bhuvi vs Siraj may decide the contest
content: Amid the Virat Kohli–Shubman Gill spotlight and Dharamsala’s batting-friendly conditions, IPL Qualifier 1 could be decided not by the big hitters but by the bowlers. With both Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bengaluru boasting disciplined and in-form attacks featuring Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Mohammed Siraj, Kagiso Rabada and Rashid Khan, the battle for early breakthroughs and composure in key overs may determine who books a direct ticket to the final.

title: 'People can move freely': Pune top cop fact-checks 'curfew' buzz, says special prohibitory orders
content: Pune police are cracking down on unauthorized hawkers and eateries violating late-night operating norms, with many establishments now required to close by 10 pm. This move aims to curb criminal activity in popular late-night spots. Meanwhile, Pimpri Chinchwad has issued prohibitory orders against carrying weapons and inciting public disorder.

title: Veteran actor Ramakant Dayama passes away; fellow TV star Shubhangi Latkar confirms his demise
content: Veteran actor Ramakant Dayama, who was part of several popular TV shows and movies, passed away earlier today (May 26). Fellow actor Shubhangi Latkar shared an old video of him singing and dancing along with other stars. While sharing the video clip, she mourned his untimely demise. The actress also penned a long, heartfelt note paying tribute to Ramakant.

title: MI IPL 2026 autopsy: How Mumbai lost their aura, fortress and winning formula
content: Mumbai Indians endured their longest title drought, finishing ninth in IPL 2026 despite a star-studded squad. A relentless injury crisis, Jasprit Bumrah's dip in form, and poor powerplay/death bowling contributed to their disastrous campaign. Hardik Pandya's captaincy also faced heavy scrutiny as the team struggled to find stability and lost their Wankhede fortress.

title: India, Canada eye 'game changer' deal; aim to triple bilateral trade to $50 bn by 2030
content: India and Canada are accelerating free trade agreement negotiations, aiming to boost bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Both nations are committed to deepening economic ties, with a "Team Canada" business delegation set to visit India later this year to explore new opportunities.

title: 'Killed her over secret 1st marriage': Missing woman found dead in washroom; husband under scanner
content: A 22-year-old woman's decomposed body was found in a locked bathroom in IMT Manesar, days after she went missing. Police are investigating her husband, Ankit, who allegedly concealed his first marriage and children, as her family suspects he murdered her and hid the body. The investigation is ongoing, with murder charges added to the FIR.

title: Rajat Patidar: RCB's captain cool, whose strength lies in his calmness
content: From being released by Royal Challengers Bengaluru to returning as a mid-season replacement and eventually leading the franchise to its maiden IPL title, Rajat Patidar's journey has been one of persistence and patience. Built on calmness rather than flamboyance, the 32-year-old has grown into a composed leader who now stands two wins away from joining an elite list of IPL captains with back-to-back titles.

title: Fuel shock: How price hike changes what you eat, what you buy and how you travel
content: Fuel prices have risen sharply, adding to household expenses and transportation costs. This surge, driven by global events, is straining supply chains and impacting everyday goods. Oil marketing companies are seeing stock gains. The government faces a balancing act between consumer relief and revenue. The economic outlook remains a focus amidst these challenges.

title: Why Delhi Gymkhana Club’s crisis feels personal for generations of members
content: Long-time members of Delhi Gymkhana Club describe it as a second home, deeply integrated into family life, friendships, and daily routines for decades. The institution fostered a sense of community and belonging, offering a cherished space for recreation, personal refuge, and cultural expression.

title: Quote of the day by Sean Penn
content: Sean Penn, a celebrated actor, openly questions the allure of stardom, calling celebrity culture an "obscene disease." Despite achieving immense success, he expresses a desire to "turn one's back on stardom," viewing it as a form of common sense. Penn's candid reflections reveal his discomfort with Hollywood's machinery and his pursuit of authenticity over fame.

title: ‘Gag order’ in Malayalam film body AMMA amid controversies
content: AMMA has been rattled after its former joint secretary Ansiba Hassan levelled serious allegations against executive committee member Tini Tom

title: PFI in Kerala disbands itself?
content: A press release issued in the name of the PFI State general secretary asked cadres to halt political activities and organisation work.

title: Budget 2024: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announces special schemes for Bihar
content: The schemes for Bihar announced by Nirmala Sitharaman in Union Budget 2024-25 are part of a larger plan called ‘Purvodaya’ to facilitate development of eastern India

title: India, Canada looking at concluding trade pact talks by year end
content: India and Canada are negotiating a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA); two rounds of negotiations have been completed

title: Mallikarjun Kharge slams Modi government over fuel prices, questions lack of relief despite fall in crude oil rates
content: From transportation to food commodities, the burden of inflation on the common man continues to intensify. Despite this, the government's profiteering continues unabated, says Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge

title: Six patients killed as Jaipur hospital’s trauma centre catches fire; government appoints probe panel
content: The deceased, including two women and four men, were among the patients being treated in the neuro ICU. Attendants say the hospital staff ignored their alert about sparks and smoke 20 minutes before the fire spread. It is suspected that the fire was caused by a short circuit

title: AIADMK, BJP criticise Tamil Nadu government over crop loan waiver announcement
content: Mr. Palaniswami said the TVK, in its election manifesto, assured to completely waive off loans of farmers who held up to 5 acre of land. But now, instead of using land as the criterion, the government has considered money as the yardstick.

title: Indian Army constructs 190-ft-long Bailey bridge at Wayanad to connect landslide affected areas
content: The construction of the bridge started at 9.30 p.m. on July 31 and was completed by 5.30 p.m. on August 1.

title: Markapuram bus fire: Telangana sets up control rooms in Nirmal, Jagtial
content: The incident was reported on March 26 in Markapuram district, when a lorry rammed into a private passenger bus, triggering a massive blaze that rapidly engulfed both vehicles

title: Punjab civic polls: 13.5% voter turnout recorded till 10 am
content: Polling through ballot papers began at 8 a.m. and will continue till 5 p.m; the counting of votes will take place on May 29

title: Kuwait fire: Kannur, Kasaragod natives from Kerala among those killed in blaze
content: The blaze claimed at least 49 lives, about 40 of whom were Indian nationals, and left 50 others injured.

title: Ending uncertainty, Suresh Gopi signals he will continue as Minister in Modi 3.0 government
content: Actor-turned-politician Suresh Gopi has seemingly flip-flopped on his earlier assertion that BJP’s national leadership would relieve him of his ministerial responsibility and permit him to honour his cinema commitments

title: Supreme Court declares electoral bonds scheme unconstitutional
content: Amendments to the law, which allowed companies to make unlimited financial contributions to political parties under the cover of absolute anonymity, declared ‘manifestly arbitrary’

title: Govt. will work to make Assam ₹10 lakh crore economy by 2028, says CM Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: ‘Tabling of the UCC bill proves our government's commitment to work for the rights of women belonging to the minority community,’ says Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma

title: Masjids turn learning hubs: innovative education initiative empowering local students
content: In Akbarnagar area of Hyderabad’s Old City, mosques have been transformed into part-time schools, or maktabs, teaching subjects like math, science, English and Telugu. This initiative, started by a group of techies offers free education to children from local government and budget schools

title: Dial 1912 for power complaints during heatwave, says Andhra Pradesh Energy Minister
content: Energy Minister Gottipati Ravi Kumar asked consumers to dial the toll-free 1912 helpline for power complaints during heatwave-related outages across Andhra Pradesh

title: Supreme Court notice to Centre on plea highlighting vacancies in Income Tax Appellate Tribunal
content: Senior advocate Mukul Rohatgi, appearing for the petitioner, submitted all posts of officers, including registrars, that are lying vacant for a number of years

title: EC announces biennial elections for Legislative Council seats in Bihar
content: The commission also announced a bypoll for the Legislative Council seat vacated by former Chief Minister Nitish Kumar following his resignation

title: Dera chief Ram Rahim granted parole for 16th time since 2017 conviction
content: The popular Dera Sacha Sauda chief has been granted a 30-day parole; Sikh organisations have previously criticised the repeated grant of parole and furlough to Ram Rahim

title: Tamil Nadu government announces cooperative crop loan waiver up to ₹50,000
content: As the loan amount increases, the quantum of the amount waived will decrease; small and marginal farmers who borrowed more than ₹1 lakh will be provided a flat waiver of ₹5,000

title: Quad Foreign Ministers meet LIVE: Quad ramps up Indo-Pacific surveillance, critical mineral cooperation
content: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announces launch of Indo-Pacific Maritime Surveillance Co-operation initiative

title: Another blow to AIADMK as fourth MLA Esakki Subaya resigns post in Tamil Nadu
content: Meanwhile, AIADMK legislators belonging to the faction led by party general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami met the Speaker and submitted a petition requesting him to withdraw the acceptance of the resignations tendered by three of the party’s MLAs

title: CNG prices hiked fourth time in eleven days in Delhi and North India; latest by ₹2/kg
content: CNG in the National Capital territory of Delhi now costs ₹83.09 per kg; prices have effectively risen ₹6 per kilogram spanning across four tranches

title: India, U.S. strike Critical Minerals deal amid China concerns
content: The framework on securing supplies of mining and processing of critical minerals was signed on the sidelines of the Quad Foreign Ministers' meeting in New Delhi

title: Kerala woman delivers baby in hospital toilet; newborn rescued after being thrown out of window
content: A doctor said a 19-year-old woman, reportedly unmarried, had approached the hospital in Alappuzha seeking treatment for stomach pain, but refused to undergo a physical examination. This raised suspicion that the woman might be pregnant

title: Police attach ₹1.25 crore property of alleged drug peddler in Jammu and Kashmir’s Kathua
content: “The attached property comprises shops and a dhaba located at Chapper Morh in Hiranagar,” officials said

title: Quad Foreign Ministers meet: Indo-Pacific must remain driver for global growth: Jaishankar
content: In his opening remarks at the Quad Foreign Ministers' meeting, EAM S. Jaishankar specifically called for "trusted and transparent" partnerships to bring peace and prosperity to the Indo-Pacific

title: Ahead of crucial Congress meeting, Karnataka CM has breakfast with loyalists in Delhi
content: The Congress high command has convened a meeting with Mr. Siddaramaiah and Deputy CM D. K. Shivakumar at the party headquarters in New Delhi

title: Facing a power cut? Here’s how to complain in your city
content: Reports of power cuts from several parts of India are surfacing. Here is how to report power cuts and know the status of your complaint.

title: Kerala school fitness: Local Self-Government dept engineers exempted from school wiring inspections
content: Meeting chaired by General Education Minister decides that licensed wiremen can inspect the wiring; order yet to be issued

title: Three fishermen rescued, one missing after fibre boat capsizes off Kodiyakarai coast
content: Three fishermen who were holding on to the overturned boat were rescued by Kodiyakarai fishermen and brought to the shore on Tuesday morning

title: Supreme Court refuses urgent hearing of PIL seeking complete ban on cow slaughter
content: The plea seeks directions to enforce anti-slaughter laws to protect the cow and its progeny from slaughter

title: Missing sheets in evaluated papers leave city’s Class 12 CBSE students in shock
content: CBSE’s first nationwide rollout of the On-Screen Marking (OSM) system for Class 12 evaluation has come under public scrutiny after students from several cities began posting about alleged discrepancies

title: What is the Supreme Court’s clarification on sedition law? | Explained
content: The Supreme Court has allowed sedition cases to resume, but only if the accused agrees. What does this mean for Section 124A, which was put on hold in 2022? Here’s a breakdown of the law, the controversy, and the latest developments.

title: NGO asks Chief Justice of India Surya Kant to withdraw remarks against environmentalists
content: They pointed out that the Constitution of India recognises the importance of environmental protection

title: Vellore TVK functionary removed from party post over bribery charges by brick kiln operators
content: A group led by T.R. Vijayakumar had reportedly demanded that each brick kiln operator pay a bribe of ₹1 lakh every month to run their units smoothly without any political interference

title: Integrated processing  facility for CURE’s waste to be set up in  Rangareddy’s Siddapur
content: Establishment of the facility is not likely to cut down the waste arriving at Jawahar Nagar site, but it could reduce the burden on the latter, by speeding up the processing.

title: Coalition govt. put Andhra Pradesh back on growth path: TDP
content: Minister says investments worth nearly ₹20 lakh crore were brought to the State in two years; attacks Jagan for making ‘baseless allegations’ against the government

title: Ebola screening begins at Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada airports; no case found so far
content: Travellers with a recent African travel history are being tracked at the two airports and the Visakhapatnam port, and a 30-bed isolation ward is ready for any suspected case

title: Canada PM's visit to India built strong foundation for truly renewed partnership: Modi
content: The Canadian Prime Minister was in India from February 27 to March 2, during which India and Canada sealed key pacts on supplies of Uranium and critical minerals and agreed to conclude a comprehensive economic partnership agreement soon.

title: Congress national youth wing secretary Srinidhi resigns after AIADMK MLAs join TVK
content: The move of the Congress’ post-poll ally TVK to induct three AIADMK MLAs, who had exited the NDA, despite the Congress having maintained that it would not align with divisive forces, has raised serious concerns about consistency and clarity of political principles within the national party, she said

title: Canada PM Carney’s visit to India reset ties after 2023 killing of Sikh activist, Piyush Goyal says
content: The ties were strained under Mark Carney's predecessor in the wake of the 2023 killing of Sikh activist Hareep Singh Nijjar in Canada

title: T.N. CM Vijay allows five shows a day for new films
content: Theatres are allowed to run five daily shows on local festival days, public holidays, Saturdays, and Sundays

title: War of words intensified in U.P. over power cuts in peak summer
content: The government failed to take immediate measures to address the hardships and difficulties faced by the public regarding electricity supply, says Samajwadi Party spokesperson Ammeque Jamei

title: Gurmitkal MLA raises drinking water issue with Yadgir DC
content: The MLA also said that people in rural areas are facing power cuts due to the recent spell of rain and winds, as the GESCOM failed to restore electricity supply even after 24 hours of disruption

title: Heatwave warning for 16 Telangana districts on May 26
content: Additionally, warm night conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Rangareddy, Hyderabad and Medchal Malkajgiri districts

title: UDF government will scrutinise steps taken by previous LDF government in MSC Elsa 3 disaster, says V.D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister says government closely monitoring fuel price hike and its possible impact on common man

title: Farmers are an integral part of alco-bev industry, do not leave them behind: Industry expert
content: Most raw materials required for breweries — including barley, grains, fruits, and other agricultural produce — are cultivated by farmers

title: Nitin Nabin sends BJP State unit into introspective mode
content: Sending a message of unity to the party State unit, which has been gripped by groupism for over a decade, he asked the leaders to protect the interests of the organisation rather than individuals

title: CBI takes over probe in Twisha Sharma death case
content: After the meeting, the CBI re-registered the state police FIR as its own case, according to the procedure

title: Drunken brawl turns deadly: Labourer beaten to death near construction site, accused arrested
content: When family members reached the spot, they found Mehboob Basha lying unconscious with visible injuries on his body and fingers; he was later declared dead

title: Over 300 tourists stranded in Gulmarg’s high-altitude cable cars rescued
content: Panic gripped the tourists stranded in 65 gondolas after a technical slag, even as the weather turned inclement, with strong winds and rains lashing Gulmarg; the rescue operation lasted over four hours; 15 SDRF teams were deployed

title: Centre offering Ladakh unique governance model, demand for Statehood to continue: Leh Apex Body leader
content: Cherring Dorjay Lakruk, co-convenor of the Leh Apex Body, says, “We are not abandoning our demand for Statehood. Even if at present, Statehood is not possible, we will continue with the demand.”

title: Arrests, mass resignations, ‘bulldozer action’ deepen Trinamool’s crisis across West Bengal
content: Drives against illegal construction, notices to properties linked to senior Trinamool leaders, and mass resignations in local bodies pile pressure on the TMC three weeks after the BJP assumed power in West Bengal

title: Iran Claims It Shot Down US' MQ-9 Drone Amid Peace Talks
content: The Guard's forces also claimed they "fired upon an RQ-4 drone and an intruding F-35 fighter jet", without specifying when the incidents took place.

title: Highlights: Quad Ramps Up Indo-Pacific Surveillance, Critical Mineral Cooperation
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: 'Not Acceptable': Pak's Khawaja Asif On Trump's Abraham Accords Call
content: Khawaja Asif is among the politicians who vehemently oppose any idea of normalisation of ties with the Jewish state. Last month, he called Israel a "curse for humanity", while accusing it of carrying out genocide in the region.

title: Canada "Working Fast" For "Game Changer" Trade Deal With India
content: Canada and India have been in trade talks since 2010. Talks were shut down by Ottawa in 2023 after Canadian authorities alleged that India was involved in the killing of Canadian Sikh activist Hardeep Singh Nijjar near Vancouver in June that year.

title: SpaceX IPO Buzz Grows, But History Of Big Listings Warns Investors
content: SpaceX's debut is expected to be followed by OpenAI and Anthropic, tapping into demand for AI-related companies

title: "I'm Jealous Of Vijay": Pawan Kalyan Quips He Roamed Streets For 15 Years
content: Vijay, who launched his party TVK in 2024, secured 108 seats and became the largest party in 2026 Tamil Nadu elections.

title: 'Scared' Arunachal Man Sought Cop's Help, Then Found Dead Near Cemetery
content: Mophuk had contacted his daughter, Ngona Mophuk, and informed her that some unknown persons were allegedly following him and that he was scared.

title: On CCTV, Woman From Gujarat Shot Dead At US Supermarket
content: The woman, who had been working at that general store in Virginia for the last 10 years, has been identified as Meghnaben Patel. She was 45.

title: Eye On China, Quad Expands Cooperation On Minerals And Maritime Security
content: Quad countries announce critical minerals framework and maritime security initiatives in Indo-Pacific.

title: Fake Brides, Real Betrayal: 42 Families Duped In Madhya Pradesh Marriage Scam
content: The grooms arrived, the families waited, and the rituals were expected to begin, but the brides never came.

title: Fuel, Extreme Heat, El Nino: Why India "May Have A Terrible Year Ahead"
content: A weak monsoon, soaring temperatures, costly crude oil and food inflation could combine to squeeze households, businesses and economic growth in 2026.

title: US-Iran War LIVE: US Says Iran War Disagreements Down To "A Word, A Sentence"
content: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Highlights: "Will Knock Them Out A Lot Harder": Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India-US Reset At Quad: Why Rubio's Visit Could Be A Win-Win, And A Game Changer
content: The visit's argument, stated and unstated, is that the US and India do not need to agree on everything to benefit from each other's momentum. They need, instead, to align on the parts of the map where alignment matters most.

title: 'Perpetual Bad Things': Experts Decode Why 40% People Are Avoiding News
content: Globally, 40 per cent report they at least sometimes or often do the same, the highest figure ever recorded. People shared consistent reasons for this: the news put them in a bad mood, they felt overwhelmed and powerless to act.

title: Second Autopsy Ordered After Tiger Death Near Bandhavgarh Reserve
content: A second autopsy is conducted on a tiger that died near Bandhavgarh after tranquilisation raised questions.
